And He sent neḇi'im to them, to bring them back to HWHY. And they witnessed against them, but they did not listen.

Then the Ruach of Aluahim came upon Zeḵaryah son of Yehoyaḏa the kohen, who stood above the people and said to them, “Thus said Aluahim, ‘Why are you transgressing the commands of HWHY and do not prosper? Because you have forsaken HWHY, He has forsaken you.’ ”
And they conspired against him and at the command of the sovereign they stoned him with stones in the courtyard of the House of HWHY.
Thus Yo’ash the sovereign did not remember the kindness which Yehoyaḏa his father had done to him and slew his son. And as he died, he said, “HWHY does see and repays!”

And it came to be, at the turn of the year, that the army of Aram came up against him. And they came into Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim and destroyed all the rulers of the people from among the people and sent all their spoil to the sovereign of Damascus.
For the army of Aram came with few men, but HWHY gave a very great army into their hand, because they had forsaken HWHY Aluahim of their fathers. So they executed mishpat against Yo’ash.

And when they had withdrawn from him – for they left him very sick – his own servants conspired against him because of the blood of the sons of Yehoyaḏa the kohen and slew him on his bed and he died. And they buried him in the City of Dawiḏ, but they did not bury him in the tombs of the sovereigns.
And these are the ones who conspired against him: Zaḇaḏ the son of Shim‛ath the Ammonitess and Yehozaḇaḏ the son of Shimrith the Mo’aḇitess.

As to his sons and the many words about him and the rebuilding of the House of Aluahim, see, they are written in the commentary of the book of the sovereigns. And Amatsyahu his son reigned in his place.
Amatsyahu was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and he reigned twenty-nine years in Yerushalayim. And the name of his mother was Yehoaddan of Yerushalayim.
And he did what was right in the eyes of HWHY, but not with a perfect heart.
And it came to be, when the reign was established for him, that he slew his servants who had smitten his father the sovereign.

But he did not put their children to death, but did as it is written in the Torah in the Book of Mosheh, where HWHY commanded, saying, “Fathers are not put to death for their children and children are not put to death for their fathers, but each one has to die for his own sin.”

And Amatsyahu gathered Yahuḏah and set over them commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, according to the fathers’ houses, for all Yahuḏah and Binyamin. And he registered them from twenty years old and above and found them to be three hundred thousand choice men going out to the army, handling spear and shield.
And he hired one hundred thousand mighty brave ones from Yasharal for one hundred talents of silver.
But a man of Aluahim came to him, saying, “O sovereign, do not let the army of Yasharal go with you, for HWHY is not with Yasharal, with all the children of Ephrayim.
“But if you are going, do it! Be strong in battle, Alse Aluahim would make you fall before the enemy, for Aluahim has power to help and to overthrow.”

And Amatsyahu said to the man of Aluahim, “But what do we do about the hundred talents which I have given to the army of Yasharal?” And the man of Aluahim answered, “HWHY has more to give you than this.”
So Amatsyahu dismissed the army that had come to him from Ephrayim, to go back home. And they were greatly enraged against Yahuḏah and they returned home in a rage.
And Amatsyahu strengthened himself and led his people and went to the Valley of Salt and smote ten thousand of the sons of Sĕ‛ir.
And the sons of Yahuḏah took captive another ten thousand alive and they brought them to the top of the rock and threw them down from the top of the rock and all of them were dashed to pieces.

And the soldiers of the army which Amatsyahu had sent back from going with him to battle, they raided the cities of Yahuḏah from Shomeron to Bĕyth Ḥoron and smote three thousand in them and took much spoil.

And it came to be, after Amatsyahu came from smiting the Eḏomites, that he brought the mighty ones of the people of Sĕ‛ir and set them up to be his mighty ones and bowed down before them and burned incense to them.

Therefore the displeasure of HWHY burned against Amatsyahu and He sent him a naḇi who said to him, “Why have you sought the mighty ones of the people, which did not deliver their own people from your hand?”

And it came to be, as he talked with him, that the sovereign said to him, “Have we appointed you counsellor to the sovereign? Stop! Why should they smite you?” Then the naḇi stopped and said, “I know that Aluahim has counselled to destroy you, because you have done this and have not listened to my counsel.”
And Amatsyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah took counsel and sent to Yo’ash son of Yeho’aḥaz, son of Yĕhu, sovereign of Yasharal, saying, “Come, let us look each other in the face!”

And Yo’ash sovereign of Yasharal sent to Amatsyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah, saying, “The thistle that was in Leḇanon sent to the cedar that was in Leḇanon, saying, ‘Give your daughter to my son as wife.’ And a wild beast that was in Leḇanon passed by and trampled the thistle.
“You have said, ‘See, I have smitten Eḏom,’ and your heart has lifted you up to boast. Now stay at home, why should you stir up yourself to evil, that you should fall and Yahuḏah with you?”
But Amatsyahu did not listen, for it came from Aluahim, in order to give them into the hand of their enemies, because they had sought the mighty ones of Eḏom.
So Yo’ash sovereign of Yasharal went out. And he and Amatsyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah faced one another at Bĕyth Shemesh, which belongs to Yahuḏah.
And Yahuḏah was smitten before Yasharal and they each fled to his tent.

And Yo’ash the sovereign of Yasharal caught Amatsyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah, son of Yo’ash, son of Yeho’aḥaz, at Bĕyth Shemesh and brought him to Yerushalayim and broke down the wall of Yerushalayim from the Gate of Ephrayim to the Corner Gate, four hundred cubits,
and took all the gold and the silver and all the utensils that were found in the House of Aluahim with Oḇĕḏ-Eḏom and the treasures of the sovereign’s house and hostages and returned to Shomeron.
And Amatsyahu son of Yo’ash, sovereign of Yahuḏah, lived fifteen years after the death of Yo’ash son of Yeho’aḥaz, sovereign of Yasharal.
And the rest of the acts of Amatsyahu, from the first to the last, see, are they not written in the book of the sovereigns of Yahuḏah and Yasharal?
And from the time that Amatsyahu turned away from following HWHY, they made a conspiracy against him in Yerushalayim and he fled to Laḵish. And they sent after him to Laḵish and killed him there,
and they brought him on horses and buried him with his fathers in the City of Yahuḏah.
And all the people of Yahuḏah took Uzziyahu, who was sixteen years old and set him up to reign instead of his father Amatsyahu.
He built Ĕyloth and restored it to Yahuḏah, after the sovereign slept with his fathers.
Uzziyahu was sixteen years old when he began to reign and he reigned fifty-two years in Yerushalayim. And his mother’s name was Yeḵolyah of Yerushalayim.
And he did what was right in the eyes of HWHY, according to all that his father Amatsyahu did.
And he sought Aluahim in the days of Zeḵaryahu, who had understanding in the visions of Aluahim. And while he sought HWHY, Aluahim made him prosper.
And he went out and fought against the Philistines and broke down the wall of Gath and the wall of Yaḇneh and the wall of Ashdoḏ and built cities around Ashdoḏ and among the Philistines.
And Aluahim helped him against the Philistines and against the Araḇians who lived in Gur Ba‛al and the Me‛unites.
And the Ammonites gave gifts to Uzziyahu. And his name spread as far as the entrance of Mitsrayim, for he strengthened himself greatly.
And Uzziyahu built towers in Yeru shalayim at the Corner Gate and at the Valley Gate and at the corner buttress and strengthened them.

And he built towers in the wilderness and dug many wells, for he had much livestock, both in the low country and in the plain, farmers and vinedressers in the mountains and in Karmel, for he loved the soil.

And Uzziyah had an army of fighting men who went out to battle by divisions, according to the number on their roll as prepared by Ye‛i’Al the scribe and Ma‛asĕyahu the officer, under the hand of Ḥananyahu, one of the sovereign’s commanders.
The total number of the clan chiefs of the mighty brave ones was two thousand six hundred.
And under their hand was an army of three hundred and seven thousand five hundred that fought with mighty power, to help the sovereign against the enemy.
And Uzziyahu prepared for them, for the entire army, shields and spears and helmets and body armour and bows and sling stones.

And he made machines in Yerushalayim, devised by skilled men, to be on the towers and the corners, to shoot arrows and large stones. And his name spread far and wide, for he was marvellously helped til he became strong.
But when he became strong his heart was lifted up, to his destruction, for he trespassed against HWHY his Aluahim by entering the Hĕḵal of HWHY to burn incense on the mizbe'ach of incense.
And Azaryahu the kohen went in after him and with him were eighty kohanim of HWHY, who were brave men.

And they stood up against Sovereign Uzziyahu and said to him, “It is not for you, Uzziyahu, to burn incense to HWHY, but for the kohanim, the sons of Aharon, who are set-apart to burn incense. Get out of the set-apart place, for you have trespassed and there is no esteem to you from HWHY Aluahim.”

And Uzziyahu was wroth. And he had a censer in his hand to burn incense. And while he was wroth with the kohanim, leprosy broke out on his forehead, before the kohanim in the House of HWHY, beside the incense mizbe'ach.

And Azaryahu the chief kohen and all the kohanim looked at him and saw that he was leprous on his forehead. And they hurried him from there. And he also hurried to get out, because HWHY had struck him.

And Sovereign Uzziyahu was a leper until the day of his death and dwelt in a seperate house, because he was a leper, for he was cut off from the House of HWHY. And Yotham his son was over the sovereign’s house, ruling the people of the land.
And the rest of the acts of Uzziyahu, from the first to the last, the naḇi Yeshayahu the son of Amots wrote.

So Uzziyahu slept with his fathers and they buried him with his fathers in the field of burial which belonged to the sovereigns, for they said, “He is a leper.” And Yotham his son reigned in his place.
Yotham was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim. And his mother’s name was Yerushah the daughter of Tsaḏoq.
And he did what was right in the eyes of HWHY, according to all that his father Uzziyahu had done. Only, he did not come into the Hĕḵal of HWHY. And the people continued to act corruptly.
He built the Upper Gate of the House of HWHY and he built much on the wall of Ophel.
And he built cities in the hill country of Yahuḏah and in the forests he built palaces and towers.

And he fought with the sovereign of the Ammonites and prevailed over them. And the children of Ammon gave him in that year one hundred talents of silver and ten thousand kors of wheat and ten thousand of barley. This is what the children of Ammon paid him, also in the second and third years.
And Yotham strengthened himself, for he prepared his ways before HWHY his Aluahim.
And the rest of the acts of Yotham and all his battles and his ways, see, they are written in the book of the sovereigns of Yasharal and Yahuḏah.
He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim.
So Yotham slept with his fathers and they buried him in the City of Dawiḏ. And his son Aḥaz reigned in his place.
Aḥaz was twenty years old when he began to reign and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim. And he did not do what was right in the eyes of HWHY, as his father Dawiḏ had done,
and walked in the ways of the sovereigns of Yasharal and made moulded images for the Ba‛als.

And he himself burned incense in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom and burned his children in the fire, according to the abominations of the gentiles whom HWHY dispossessed from before the children of Yasharal,
and slaughtered and burned incense on the high places and on the hills and under every green tree.

Therefore HWHY his Aluahim gave him into the hand of the sovereign of Aram and they smote him and took many of them away as captives and brought them to Damascus. And he was also given into the hand of the sovereign of Yasharal, who smote him with a great slaughter.
And Peqaḥ son of Remalyahu slew one hundred and twenty thousand in Yahuḏah in one day, all brave men, because they had forsaken HWHY Aluahim of their fathers.
And Ziḵri, a mighty man of Ephrayim, slew Ma‛asĕyahu son of the sovereign and Azriqam the officer over the house and Alqanah who was second to the sovereign.
And the children of Yasharal took captive from their brothers two hundred thousand women, sons and daughters. And they also seized from them much spoil and they brought the spoil to Shomeron.

But a naḇi of HWHY was there, whose name was Oḏĕḏ. And he went out before the army that came to Shomeron and said to them, “See, because HWHY Aluahim of your fathers was displeased with Yahuḏah, He has given them into your hand and you have slain them in a rage that reaches up to the shamayim.
“And now you are planning to make the children of Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim your male and female slaves, but are you not also guilty before HWHY your Aluahim?
“Now therefore, listen to me and return the captives whom you have taken captive from your brothers, for the heat of the wrath of HWHY is upon you.”

And some of the heads of the children of Ephrayim: Azaryahu son of Yehoḥanan, Bereḵyahu son of Meshillĕmoth and Yeḥizqiyahu son of Shallum and Amasa son of Ḥaḏlai, stood up against those who came from the army,

and said to them, “Do not bring the captives here, to bring on us guilt before HWHY. Are you planning to add to our sins and to our guilt? For our guilt is great and burning is the wrath on Yasharal.”
And the armed men left the captives and the spoil before the leaders and all the qahal.

And the men who were designated by name rose up and took the captives and from the spoil they put on all the naked among them, dressed them and gave them sandals and gave them food and drink and anointed them and let all the weak ones ride on donkeys and brought them to their brothers at Yeriḥo, the city of palm trees, then returned to Shomeron.
At that time Sovereign Aḥaz sent to the sovereigns of Ashshur to help him.
For the Eḏomites had come again and smote Yahuḏah and took away captives.

And the Philistines invaded the cities of the low country and of the South of Yahuḏah and captured Bĕyth Shemesh and Ayalon and Geḏĕroth and Soḵo with its villages and Timnah with its villages and Gimzo with its villages and dwelt there.
For HWHY had brought Yahuḏah low because of Aḥaz sovereign of Yasharal, for he brought about a lack of restraint in Yahuḏah and trespassed against HWHY.
And Tiglath-Pileser the sovereign of Ashshur came against him and distressed him and did not help him,
though Aḥaz had taken some of the treasures from the House of HWHY, from the house of the sovereign and from the leaders and he gave it to the sovereign of Ashshur, but he did not help him.
And in the time of his distress Sovereign Aḥaz trespassed even more against HWHY– this Sovereign Aḥaz –

and he slaughtered to the mighty ones of Damascus, those smiting him, saying, “Because the mighty ones of the sovereigns of Aram help them, I offer to them and they help me.” But they were to cause him and all Yasharal to stumble.

And Aḥaz gathered the utensils of the House of Aluahim and cut in pieces the utensils of the House of Aluahim and shut the doors of the House of HWHY and made for himself mizbe'achot in every corner of Yerushalayim.
And in every city, even the cities of Yahuḏah, he made high places to burn incense to other mighty ones and provoked HWHY Aluahim of his fathers.
And the rest of his acts and all his ways, from first to last, see, they are written in the book of the sovereigns of Yahuḏah and Yasharal.
So Aḥaz slept with his fathers and they buried him in the city, in Yerushalayim, but they did not bring him into the tombs of the sovereigns of Yasharal. And Ḥizqiyahu his son reigned in his place.
Ḥizqiyahu began to reign when he was twenty-five years old and he reigned twenty-nine years in Yerushalayim. And his mother’s name was Aḇiyah the daughter of Zeḵaryahu.
And he did what was right in the eyes of HWHY, according to all that his father Dawiḏ did.
In the first year of his reign, in the first month, he opened the doors of the House of HWHY and repaired them,
and brought in the kohanim and the Lĕwites and gathered them in the open place to the east.
And he said to them, “Listen to me, O Lĕwites! Now set yourselves apart, set-apart the House of HWHY Aluahim of your fathers and remove the uncleanness from the set-apart place.
“For our fathers have trespassed and have done evil in the eyes of HWHY our Aluahim and have forsaken Him and have turned their faces away from the Dwelling Place of HWHY and have given their backs.
“And they have shut the doors of the porch and put out the lamps and they have not burned incense or offered burnt offerings in the set-apart place to the Aluahim of Yasharal.
“Therefore the wrath of HWHY fell upon Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim and He has given them up for a trembling, for an astonishment and for a hissing, as you see with your eyes.
“And see, because of this our fathers have fallen by the sword. And our sons, daughters and our wives are in captivity for this.
“Now it is in my heart to make a berith with HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal, so that the heat of His wrath turns away from us.
“My sons, do not be slack, for HWHY has chosen you to stand before Him, to serve Him and to be attendants for Him and burn incense.”

And the Lĕwites rose up: Maḥath son of Amasai and Yo’Al son of Azaryahu, of the sons of the Qehathites; and of the sons of Merari, Qish son of Aḇdi and Azaryahu son of Yehallel’Al; and of the Gĕreshonites, Yo’aḥ son of Zimmah and Ĕḏen son of Yo’aḥ;
and of the sons of Alitsaphan, Shimri and Ye‛i’Al; and of the sons of Asaph, Zeḵaryahu and Mattanyahu;
and of the sons of Hĕman, Yeḥi’Al and Shim‛i; and of the sons of Yeḏuthun, Shemayah and Uzzi’Al.
And they gathered their brothers and set themselves apart and went according to the command of the sovereign, at the words of HWHY, to cleanse the House of HWHY.

And the kohanim came into the inner part of the House of HWHY to cleanse it and brought out all the uncleanness they found in the Hĕḵal of HWHY to the courtyard of the House of HWHY. Then the Lĕwites received it to take it outside to the wadi Qiḏron.

And they began to set-apart on the first day of the first month and on the eighth day of the month they came to the porch of HWHY. And they set-apart the House of HWHY in eight days and on the sixteenth day of the first month they had finished.

Then they came in to Sovereign Ḥizqiyahu and said, “We have cleansed all the House of HWHY and the mizbe'ach of burnt offerings with all its utensils and the table of the showbread with all its utensils.
“And all the utensils which Sovereign Aḥaz in his reign had pushed aside, when he trespassed, we have prepared and set-apart. And see, they are before the mizbe'ach of HWHY.”
And Sovereign Ḥizqiyahu rose up early and gathered the heads of the city and went up to the House of HWHY.

And they brought seven bulls and seven rams and seven lambs and seven male goats for a sin offering for the reign, for the set-apart place and for Yahuḏah. And he said to the kohanim, the sons of Aharon, to offer them on the mizbe'ach of HWHY.

So they slaughtered the bulls and the kohanim received the blood and sprinkled it on the mizbe'ach. And they slaughtered the rams and sprinkled the blood on the mizbe'ach. And they slaughtered the lambs and sprinkled the blood on the mizbe'ach.
And they brought out the male goats of the sin offering before the sovereign and the qahal and they laid their hands on them,

and the kohanim slaughtered them and with their blood made a sin offering on the mizbe'ach, to make an atonement for all Yasharal, for the sovereign said that the burnt offering and the sin offering is for all Yasharal.

And he appointed the Lĕwites in the House of HWHY with cymbals, with harps and with lyres, according to the command of Dawiḏ and of Gaḏ, seer of the sovereign and of Nathan the naḇi, for the command was by the hand of HWHY, by the hand of His neḇi'im.
And the Lĕwites stood with the instruments of Dawiḏ and the kohanim with the trumpets.

And Ḥizqiyahu gave the order to offer the burnt offering on the mizbe'ach. And when the burnt offering began, the singing unto HWHY began, with the trumpets and with the instruments of Dawiḏ sovereign of Yasharal.
And all the qahal were bowing and the singers singing and the trumpeters blowing – all this until the burnt offering was completed.
And at the completion of the offering, the sovereign and all who were present with him bowed and worshipped.

And Sovereign Ḥizqiyahu and the rulers ordered the Lĕwites to sing praise to HWHY with the words of Dawiḏ and of Asaph the seer. And they sang praises with gladness and they bowed their heads and worshipped.

And Ḥizqiyahu responded and said, “Now that you have ordained yourselves to HWHY, come near and bring slaughterings and thank offerings into the House of HWHY.” And the qahal brought in slaughterings and thank offerings and all those whose hearts were so moved brought burnt offerings.
And the number of the burnt offerings which the qahal brought was seventy bulls, one hundred rams, two hundred lambs – all these for a burnt offering to HWHY.
And the set-apart gifts were six hundred bulls and three thousand sheep.

Only, the kohanim were too few and were unable to skin all the burnt offerings, so their brothers the Lĕwites helped them until the work was completed and until the other kohanim had set themselves apart, for the Lĕwites were more upright of heart to set themselves apart, than the kohanim.
And there were also many burnt offerings, with the fat of the shalom offerings and with the drink offerings for every burnt offering. Thus the service of the House of HWHY was re- established.
And Ḥizqiyahu and all the people rejoiced that Aluahim had prepared the people, because the matter came about so suddenly.
And Ḥizqiyahu sent to all Yasharal and Yahuḏah and he also wrote letters to Ephrayim and Menashsheh, to come to the House of HWHY at Yerushalayim, to perform the Pesach to HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal.
But the sovereign and his leaders and all the qahal in Yerushalayim had taken counsel to perform the Pesach in the second month.
For they were unable to perform it at its time, because not enough kohanim had set themselves apart and the people had not gathered at Yerushalayim.
And the matter was right in the eyes of the sovereign and in the eyes of all the qahal.

And they settled the matter, to send a call to all Yasharal, from Be’ĕrsheḇa to Dan, to come to perform a Pesach to HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal at Yerushalayim, since they had not done it for a long time, as it is written.

And the runners went to all Yasharal and Yahuḏah with the letters from the sovereign and his leaders and spoke according to the command of the sovereign, “Children of Yasharal, turn back to HWHY Aluahim of Aḇrahim, Yitsḥaq and Yasharal, so that He returns to the remnant of you who have escaped from the hand of the sovereigns of Ashshur.
“And do not be like your fathers and be like your brothers, who trespassed against HWHY Aluahim of their fathers, so that He gave them up to ruin, as you see.

“Now do not stiffen your neck, like your fathers, stretch forth the hand to HWHY and come to His set-apart place, which He has set-apart forever and serve HWHY your Aluahim, so that His burning wrath turns away from you.

“For if you turn back to HWHY, your brothers and your children shall be shown compassion by their captors, even to return to this land. For HWHY your Aluahim shows chen and compassion and does not turn His face from you if you turn back to Him.”
And the runners passed from city to city throughout the land of Ephrayim and Menashsheh, as far as Zeḇulun. But they were laughing at them and mocking them.
Some from Ashĕr and Menashsheh and from Zeḇulun, however, humbled themselves and came to Yerushalayim.
Also the hand of Aluahim was on Yahuḏah to give them one heart to do the command of the sovereign and the rulers, at the Word of HWHY.
And many people, a very great qahal, gathered at Yerushalayim to perform the Festival of Unleavened Bread in the second month.
And they rose up and removed the mizbe'achot that were in Yerushalayim and they removed all the incense mizbe'achot and threw them into the wadi Qiḏron.
Then they slaughtered the Pesach on the fourteenth day of the second month. And the kohanim and the Lĕwites were ashamed and set themselves apart and brought the burnt offerings to the House of HWHY.
And they stood in their place according to their right-ruling. According to the Torah of Mosheh, the man of Aluahim, the kohanim sprinkled the blood from the hand of the Lĕwites.
For many in the qahal had not set themselves apart. Therefore the Lĕwites were over the slaughter of the Pesach for everyone who was not clean, to set them apart to HWHY.

For many of the people, many from Ephrayim and Menashsheh, Yissasḵar and Zeḇulun, had not been cleansed, yet they ate the Pesach contrary to what was written. But Ḥizqiyahu prayed for them, saying, “HWHY who is good, provide atonement for everyone
who has prepared his heart to seek Aluahim, HWHY Aluahim of his fathers, though he is not cleansed according to the cleansing of the set-apart place.”
And HWHY listened to Ḥizqiyahu and healed the people.

And the children of Yasharal who were in Yerushalayim performed the Festival of Unleavened Bread seven days with great joy. And the Lĕwites and the kohanim praised HWHY day by day, with instruments of praise before HWHY.

And Ḥizqiyahu spoke to the heart of all the Lĕwites, those having good understanding concerning HWHY. So they ate what was appointed, seven days, slaughtering shalom offerings and making confession to HWHY Aluahim of their fathers.
And all the qahal took counsel to perform another seven days and they performed it another seven days with joy.

For Ḥizqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah presented to the qahal a thousand bulls and seven thousand sheep and the rulers presented to the qahal a thousand bulls and ten thousand sheep. And a great number of kohanim set themselves apart.
And all the qahal of Yahuḏah rejoiced and the kohanim and Lĕwites and all the qahal who had come from Yasharal and the sojourners who came from the land of Yasharal and those who dwelt in Yahuḏah.
And there came to be great joy in Yerushalayim, for since the days of Shelomoh son of Dawiḏ, the sovereign of Yasharal, the like of this had not been in Yerushalayim.
And the kohanim, the Lĕwites, rose and baruk the people and their voice was heard. And their prayer came up to His set-apart dwelling place, to the shamayim.

And at the completion of all this, all Yasharal who were present went out to the cities of Yahuḏah and broke down the pillars and cut down the Ashĕrim and tore down the high places and the mizbe'achot, from all Yahuḏah and Binyamin and in Ephrayim and Menashsheh, even to completion. Then all the children of Yasharal returned to their own cities, each to his possession.

And Ḥizqiyahu appointed the divisions of the kohanim and of the Lĕwites according to their divisions, each according to his service of the kohanim and Lĕwites for burnt offerings and shalom offerings, to serve and to give thanks and to praise in the gates of the camp of HWHY.

And the sovereign appointed a portion of his possessions for the burnt offerings, for the morning and evening burnt offerings and the burnt offerings for the Shabbaths and for the New Moons and for the appointed times, as it is written in the Torah of HWHY.
And he said to the people, those who dwelt in Yerushalayim, to give the portion for the kohanim and the Lĕwites, so that they are strengthened in the Torah of HWHY.

And as the word spread, the children of Yasharal brought large quantities of the first-fruits of grain and wine and oil and honey and of all the increase of the field. And they brought in the tithe of all, a large amount.

And the children of Yasharal and Yahuḏah, those who dwelt in the cities of Yahuḏah, brought the tithe of cattle and sheep. Even the tithe of set-apart gifts which were set-apart to HWHY their Aluahim were brought in and they gave heaps.
In the third month they began to pile up the heaps and they finished in the seventh month.
And Ḥizqiyahu and the leaders came and saw the heaps and they baruk HWHY and His people Yasharal.
And Ḥizqiyahu asked the kohanim and the Lĕwites about the heaps.

And Azaryahu the chief kohen, from the house of Tsaḏoq, answered him and said, “Since they began to bring the offerings into the House of HWHY, we have had enough to eat and have plenty left, for HWHY has baruk His people. And this great amount is left over.”
And Ḥizqiyahu ordered them to prepare rooms in the House of HWHY and they prepared them.
Then they brought in the contribution and the tithes and the set-apart gifts, trustworthily. And Konanyahu the Lĕwite was leader over them and Shim‛i his brother was the next.

And Yeḥi’Al and Azazyahu and Naḥath and Asah’Al and Yerimoth and Yozaḇaḏ and Ali’Al and Yismaḵyahu and Maḥath and Benayahu were overseers under the hand of Konanyahu and Shim‛i his brother, by order of Ḥizqiyahu the sovereign and Azaryahu the ruler of the House of Aluahim.
And Qorĕ son of Yimnah the Lĕwite, the keeper of the East Gate, was over the voluntary offerings to Aluahim, to distribute the offerings of HWHY and the most set-apart gifts.

And under his hand were Ĕḏen and Minyamin and Yĕshua and Shemayahu, Amaryahu and Sheḵanyahu, in the cities of the kohanim, to distribute trustworthily to their brothers by divisions, to the great as well as the small.

Besides those males from three years old and up who were written in the genealogy, they distributed to everyone who entered the House of HWHY his daily portion for the work of his service, by his division,
and to the kohanim who were written in the genealogy according to their father’s house and to the Lĕwites from twenty years old upward, by their duties in their divisions,
and to all listed in the genealogy, their little ones and their wives, their sons and daughters, all the company of them. For in their trustworthiness they set themselves apart in set-apartness.

And for the sons of Aharon the kohanim, who were in the fields of the open land of their cities, in each and every city, there were men who were called by name to distribute portions to all the males among the kohanim and to all who were listed by genealogies among the Lĕwites.
And Ḥizqiyahu did this in all Yahuḏah and he did what was good and what was right and what was true before HWHY his Aluahim.
And in every work that he began in the service of the House of Aluahim, in the Torah and in the command, to seek his Aluahim, with all his heart, he did and prospered.
After these matters and this trust worthiness, Sanḥĕriḇ sovereign of Ashshur came. And he entered Yahuḏah and encamped against the cities of defence and said to break them open to himself.
And Ḥizqiyahu saw that Sanḥĕriḇ had come and his face set to fight against Yerushalayim,
and he took counsel with his rulers and mighty men to stop the water from the springs which were outside the city. And they helped him.
And many people were gathered and they stopped all the springs and the stream that ran through the land, saying, “Why should the sovereigns of Ashshur come and find much water?”

And he strengthened himself and built up all the wall that was broken and raised it up to the towers and outside of it another wall and strengthened Millo, the City of Dawiḏ and made large numbers of weapons and shields.
And he appointed battle officers over the people and gathered them to him in the open space at the city gate and spoke to their heart, saying,
“Be strong and courageous, do not be afraid nor be cast down before the sovereign of Ashshur, nor before all the army that is with him. For with us there are more than with him.
“With him is an arm of flesh, but with us is HWHY our Aluahim, to help us and to fight our battles.” And the people leaned on the words of Ḥizqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah.

After this Sanḥĕriḇ the sovereign of Ashshur sent his servants to Yerushalayim – but he himself and all his power with him against Laḵish – to Ḥizqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah and to all Yahuḏah who were in Yerushalayim, saying,
“Thus said Sanḥĕriḇ the sovereign of Ashshur, ‘On what are you trusting, that you remain in Yerushalayim under siege?
‘Is Ḥizqiyahu not persuading you to give yourselves over to die by scarcity of food and by thirst, saying, “HWHY our Aluahim shall deliver us from the hand of the sovereign of Ashshur”?
‘Has not Ḥizqiyahu himself taken away His high places and His mizbe'achot and ordered Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim, saying, “Bow yourselves before one mizbe'ach and burn incense on it”?
‘Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the mighty ones of the nations of those lands in any way able to deliver their lands out of my hand?
‘Who was there among all the mighty ones of those nations that my fathers put under the ban that could deliver his people from my hand, that your Aluahim should be able to deliver you from my hand?

‘And now, do not let Ḥizqiyahu deceive you or persuade you like this and do not believe him; for no mighty one of any nation or reign was able to deliver his people from my hand or the hand of my fathers – much less your Aluahim to deliver you from my hand!’ ”
And his servants spoke even more against HWHY Aluahim and against His servant Ḥizqiyahu.

And he wrote letters to reproach HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal and to speak against Him, saying, “As the mighty ones of the nations of other lands have not delivered their people from my hand, so the Aluahim of Ḥizqiyahu shall not deliver His people from my hand.”
Then they called out with a loud voice in the language of Yahuḏah to the people of Yerushalayim who were on the wall, to frighten them and to trouble them, in order to capture the city.
And they spoke against the Aluahim of Yerushalayim, as against the mighty ones of the people of the earth, the work of men’s hands.
And Sovereign Ḥizqiyahu and the naḇi Yeshayahu, son of Amots, prayed about this and cried out to the shamayim.

And HWHY sent a mal'ak who cut down every mighty brave one, both the leader and the commander in the camp of the sovereign of Ashshur and he returned shamefaced to his own land and went into the house of his mighty one and there some of his own offspring caused him to fall by the sword.
Thus HWHY saved Ḥizqiyahu and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim from the hand of Sanḥĕriḇ the sovereign of Ashshur and from the hand of all others and guided them on every side.
And many brought gifts to HWHY at Yerushalayim and presents to Ḥizqiyahu sovereign of Yahuḏah and he was exalted in the eyes of all nations thereafter.
In those days Ḥizqiyahu was sick and near death and he prayed to HWHY. And He spoke to him and appointed a sign for him.
However, Ḥizqiyahu did not repay according to the good done to him, for his heart was lifted up, therefore wrath came upon him and upon Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim.
Then Ḥizqiyahu humbled himself for the pride of his heart, he and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, so that the wrath of HWHY did not come upon them in the days of Ḥizqiyahu.
And Ḥizqiyahu had much riches and esteem. And he made himself treasuries for silver and for gold and for precious stones and for spices and for shields and for all desirable utensils;
and storehouses for the harvest of grain and wine and oil and stalls for all kinds of livestock and folds for flocks.
And he made cities for himself and possessions of great numbers of flocks and herds, for Aluahim gave him much property.
And Ḥizqiyahu himself had stopped the upper outlet of the waters of Giḥon and directed them to the west side of the City of Dawiḏ. And Ḥizqiyahu prospered in all his work.
However with the envoys of the princes of Baḇel, whom they sent to ask him about the wonder that was done in the land, Aluahim left him, in order to try him, to know all that was in his heart.
And the rest of the acts of Ḥizqiyahu and his kindness, see, they are written in the vision of Yeshayahu the naḇi, son of Amots, in the book of the sovereigns of Yahuḏah and Yasharal.

So Ḥizqiyahu slept with his fathers and they buried him in the upper tombs of the sons of Dawiḏ. And all Yahuḏah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim esteemed him at his death. And Menashsheh his son reigned in his place.
Menashsheh was twelve years old when he began to reign and he reigned fifty-five years in Yerushalayim.
But he did evil in the eyes of HWHY, according to the abominations of the gentiles whom HWHY dispossessed from before the children of Yasharal.

For again he rebuilt the high places which Ḥizqiyahu his father had broken down and raised up mizbe'achot for the Ba‛als and made Ashĕrim and bowed himself to all the host of the shamayim and served them.
And he built mizbe'achot in the House of HWHY, of which HWHY had said, “In Yerushalayim is My Name, forever.”
And he built mizbe'achot for all the host of the shamayim in the two courtyards of the House of HWHY.

And he made his sons pass through the fire in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom and practised magic and used divination and witchcraft and consulted mediums and spiritists. He did much evil in the eyes of HWHY, to provoke Him.

And he placed a carved image of the idol which he had made, in the House of Aluahim, of which Aluahim had said to Dawiḏ and to Shelomoh his son, “In this house and in Yerushalayim, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Yasharal, I put My Name forever,

and no more shall I remove the foot of Yasharal from the soil which I have appointed for your fathers – only if they guard to do all that I have commanded them, according to all the Torah and the laws and the right-rulings by the hand of Mosheh.”
Thus Menashsheh led Yahuḏah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim astray, to do more evil than the gentiles whom HWHY had destroyed before the children of Yasharal.
And HWHY spoke to Menashsheh and to his people, but they did not listen.
Therefore HWHY brought upon them the commanders of the army of the sovereign of Ashshur, who captured Menashsheh with hooks, bound him with bronze shackles and made him go to Baḇel.
And when he was in distress, he sought the face of HWHY his Aluahim and humbled himself greatly before the Aluahim of his fathers,
and prayed to Him. And He was moved by his entreaty and heard his supplication and brought him back to Yerushalayim into his reign. And Menashsheh knew that HWHY was Aluahim.

And after this he built a wall outside the City of Dawiḏ on the west of Giḥon, in the wadi and as far as the entrance of the Fish Gate and it went round Ophel and he made it exceedingly high. And he put army commanders in all the walled cities of Yahuḏah.

And he removed the foreign mighty ones and the idol from the House of HWHY and all the mizbe'achot that he had built in the mount of the House of HWHY and in Yerushalayim and he threw them out of the city.
And he built the mizbe'ach of HWHY and brought shalom offerings and thank offerings on it and ordered Yahuḏah to serve HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal.
But the people were still offering on the high places, though only to HWHY their Aluahim.

And the rest of the acts of Menashsheh, his prayer to his Aluahim and the words of the seers who spoke to him in the Name of HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal, see, they are written in the book of the sovereigns of Yasharal.

And his prayer and his entreaty and all his sin and his trespass and the places where he built high places and set up the Ashĕrim and the carved images, before he was humbled, see, they are written among the words of the seers.
So Menashsheh slept with his fathers and they buried him in his own house. And his son Amon reigned in his place.
Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign and he reigned two years in Yerushalayim.
But he did evil in the eyes of HWHY, as his father Menashsheh had done. And Amon offered to all the carved images which his father Menashsheh had made and served them.
And he did not humble himself before HWHY, as his father Menashsheh had humbled himself, for Amon trespassed more and more.
And his servants conspired against him and killed him in his own house.
But the people of the land smote all those who had conspired against Sovereign Amon and the people of the land set Yoshiyahu to reign in his place.
Yoshiyahu was eight years old when he began to reign and he reigned thirty-one years in Yerushalayim.
And he did what was right in the eyes of HWHY and walked in the ways of his father Dawiḏ and did not turn aside, right or left.

And in the eighth year of his reign, while he was still young, he began to seek the Aluahim of his father Dawiḏ. And in the twelfth year he began to cleanse Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim from the high places and the Ashĕrim and the carved images and the moulded images.

And they broke down the mizbe'achot of the Ba‛als in his presence and the sun-pillars which were above them he cut down. And the Ashĕrim and the carved images and the moulded images he smashed and ground them up and strewed it on the graves of those who had offered to them.
And he burned the bones of the kohanim on their mizbe'achot and cleansed Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim,
and in the cities of Menashsheh and Ephrayim and Shim‛on, as far as Naphtali, in their ruins all around.
And he broke down the mizbe'achot and the Ashĕrim and ground the carved images into dust and cut down all the sun- pillars throughout all the land of Yasharal and returned to Yerushalayim.

In the eighteenth year of his reign, when he had cleansed the land and the House, he sent Shaphan son of Atsalyahu, Ma‛asĕyahu the head of the city and Yo’aḥ son of Yo’aḥaz the recorder, to repair the House of HWHY his Aluahim.

And they went to Ḥilqiyahu the kohen gadol and they gave the silver that was brought into the House of Aluahim, which the Lĕwites who kept the doors had gathered from the hand of Menashsheh and Ephrayim and from all the remnant of Yasharal and from all Yahuḏah and Binyamin and which they had brought back to Yerushalayim,
and they gave it in the hand of the workmen, those appointed over the House of HWHY. And they gave it to the workmen who worked in the House of HWHY, to repair and strengthen the house.
And they gave it to the craftsmen and to the builders, to buy hewn stone and timber for couplings and for beams for the houses which the sovereigns of Yahuḏah had destroyed.

And the men did the work trustworthily. And over them were appointed Yaḥath and Oḇaḏyahu the Lĕwites, of the sons of Merari and Zeḵaryah and Meshullam, of the sons of the Qehathites, to oversee; and of the Lĕwites, all of whom were skilled in instruments of song,
and over the burden bearers and overseers of all who did work in any kind of service. And of the Lĕwites were scribes and officers and gatekeepers.
And when they brought out the silver that was brought into the House of HWHY, Ḥilqiyahu the kohen found the Book of the Torah of HWHY given by Mosheh.
Then Ḥilqiyahu responded and said to Shaphan the scribe, “I have found the Book of the Torah in the House of HWHY.” And Ḥilqiyahu gave the book to Shaphan.
And Shaphan brought the book to the sovereign and brought the sovereign word, saying, “All that has been given into the hand of your servants, they are doing.
“And they have poured out the silver that was found in the House of HWHY and have given it into the hand of those appointed and the workmen.”
And Shaphan the scribe informed the sovereign, saying, “Ḥilqiyahu the kohen has given me a book.” And Shaphan read it before the sovereign.
And it came to be, when the sovereign heard the words of the Torah, that he tore his garments.
And the sovereign commanded Ḥilqiyahu, Aḥiqam son of Shaphan and Aḇdon son of Miḵah and Shaphan the scribe and Asayah a servant of the sovereign, saying,

“Go, inquire of HWHY for me and for him who is left in Yasharal and in Yahuḏah, concerning the words of the book that is found. For great is the wrath of HWHY that is poured out on us, because our fathers have not guarded the Word of HWHY, to do according to all that is written in this book.”

Then Ḥilqiyahu and those of the sovereign went to Ḥuldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum son of Toqhath, son of Ḥasrah, keeper of the wardrobe, who was dwelling in Yerushalayim in the Second Quarter. And they spoke to her about this.
And she said to them, “Thus said HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Say to the man who sent you to Me,
“Thus said HWHY, ‘See, I am bringing evil on this place and on its inhabitants, all the curses that are written in the book which they have read before the sovereign of Yahuḏah,
because they have forsaken Me and burned incense to other mighty ones, to provoke Me with all the works of their hands. Therefore My wrath is poured out on this place and is not quenched.’ ” ’
“And to the sovereign of Yahuḏah, who sent you to inquire of HWHY, say this, ‘Thus said HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal, whose words you have heard,

“Because your heart was tender and you humbled yourself before Aluahim when you heard His words against this place and against its inhabitants and you humbled yourself before Me and you tore your garments and wept before Me, I also have heard,” declares HWHY.

“See, I am gathering you to your fathers and you shall be gathered to your grave in shalom, so that your eyes would not see all the evil that I am bringing on this place and its inhabitants.” ’ ” So they brought back word to the sovereign.
And the sovereign sent and gathered all the Alders of Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim.

And the sovereign went up to the House of HWHY with all the men of Yahuḏah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim and the kohanim and the Lĕwites and all the people, both great and small. And he read in their hearing all the words of the Book of the Berith which had been found in the House of HWHY.

And the sovereign stood in his place and made a berith before HWHY, to follow HWHY and to guard His commands and His witnesses and His laws, with all his heart and all his being, to do the words of the berith that were written in this book.
And he made stand all who were present in Yerushalayim and Binyamin. And the inhabitants of Yerushalayim did according to the berith of Aluahim, the Aluahim of their fathers.

And Yoshiyahu removed all the abominations from all the lands that belonged to the children of Yasharal and made all who were present in Yasharal diligently serve HWHY their Aluahim. All his days they did not turn away from following HWHY Aluahim of their fathers.
Yoshiyahu performed a Pesach to HWHY in Yerushalayim and they slaughtered the Pesach on the fourteenth day of the first month.
And he set the kohanim in their duties and strengthened them for the service of the House of HWHY,

and said to the Lĕwites who were teaching all Yasharal, who were set-apart to HWHY, “Put the set-apart ark in the house which Shelomoh son of Dawiḏ, sovereign of Yasharal, built. It is no longer to be a burden on your shoulders. Now serve HWHY your Aluahim and His people Yasharal,
and prepare by the fathers’ houses, according to your divisions, by the writing of Dawiḏ sovereign of Yasharal and by the writing of Shelomoh his son.
“And stand in the set-apart place by the divisions of the fathers’ houses of your brothers the lay people and the portion of the father’s house of the Lĕwites.
“And slaughter the Pesach and set yourselves apart and prepare for your brothers, to do according to the Word of HWHY by the hand of Mosheh.”

And Yoshiyahu gave the lay people lambs and young goats from the flock, all for Pesach offerings for everyone present, to the number of thirty thousand and three thousand cattle – these were from the sovereign’s possessions.

And his leaders contributed a voluntary offering to the people, to the kohanim and to the Lĕwites. Ḥilqiyah and Zeḵaryahu and Yeḥi’Al, leaders of the House of Aluahim, gave to the kohanim for the Pesach offerings two thousand six hundred and three hundred cattle;
and Konanyahu, his brothers Shemayahu and Nethanĕ’l and Ḥashaḇyahu and Ye‛i’Al and Yozaḇaḏ, chiefs of the Lĕwites, gave to the Lĕwites for Pesach offerings five thousand and five hundred cattle.
And the service was prepared and the kohanim stood in their places and the Lĕwites in their divisions, according to the command of the sovereign,
and they slaughtered the Pesach. And the kohanim sprinkled out of their hands, while the Lĕwites were skinning.
And they removed the burnt offerings to give them to the divisions of the fathers’ houses of the lay people, to bring to HWHY, as it is written in the Book of Mosheh and the same with the cattle.

So they roasted the Pesach offerings with fire according to the right-ruling and they boiled the set-apart offerings in pots and in cauldrons and in bowls and brought them speedily to all the lay people.

And afterward they prepared for themselves and for the kohanim, because the kohanim, the sons of Aharon, were offering burnt offerings and fat until night. So the Lĕwites prepared for themselves and for the kohanim, the sons of Aharon.

And the singers, the sons of Asaph, were in their places, according to the command of Dawiḏ and Asaph and Hĕman and Yeḏuthun the seer of the sovereign. And the gatekeepers at each gate did not have to leave their position, because their brothers the Lĕwites prepared for them.
And all the service of HWHY was prepared that day, to perform the Pesach and to offer burnt offerings on the mizbe'ach of HWHY, according to the command of Sovereign Yoshiyahu.
And the children of Yasharal who were present performed the Pesach at that time, and the Festival of Unleavened Bread for seven days.

There had not been a Pesach performed in Yasharal like it since the days of Shemu’Al the naḇi. And none of the sovereigns of Yasharal had performed such a Pesach as Yoshiyahu performed, with the kohanim and the Lĕwites and all Yahuḏah and Yasharal who were present and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.
In the eighteenth year of the reign of Yoshiyahu this Pesach was performed.
After all this, when Yoshiyahu had prepared the House, Neḵo sovereign of Mitsrayim came up to fight against Karkemish by the Euphrates. And Yoshiyahu went out against him.

And he sent mal'akim to him, saying, “What have I to do with you, sovereign of Yahuḏah? I am not coming against you this day, but against the house with which I am fighting, for Aluahim commanded me to make haste. Leave Aluahim alone, who is with me, lest He destroy you.”

However, Yoshiyahu would not turn his face from him, but disguised himself to fight against him and did not listen to the words of Neḵo from the mouth of Aluahim and came to fight in the Valley of Meḡiddo.
And the archers shot at Sovereign Yoshiyahu. And the sovereign said to his servants, “Take me away, for I am severely wounded.”

And his servants took him out of that chariot and put him in the second chariot that he had and they brought him to Yerushalayim. And he died and was buried in one of the tombs of his fathers. And all Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim were mourning for Yoshiyahu.

And Yirmeyahu lamented for Yoshi yahu. And to this day all the singing men and the singing women speak of Yoshiyahu in their lamentations and made it a law in Yasharal. And see, they are written in the Laments.
And the rest of the acts of Yoshiyahu and his kindness, according to what was written in the Torah of HWHY,
and his acts from the first to the last, see, they are written in the book of the sovereigns of Yasharal and Yahuḏah.
And the people of the land took Yeho’aḥaz son of Yoshiyahu and set him up to reign in his father’s place in Yerushalayim.
Yeho’aḥaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign and he reigned three months in Yerushalayim.
And the sovereign of Mitsrayim turned him aside in Yerushalayim and imposed on the land a levy of one hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
And the sovereign of Mitsrayim made his brother Alyaqim sovereign over Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim and changed his Name to Yehoyaqim. And Neḵo took Yeho’aḥaz his brother and brought him to Mitsrayim.
Yehoyaqim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and he reigned Aleven years in Yerushalayim. And he did evil in the eyes of HWHY his Aluahim.
Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar sovereign of Baḇel came up against him and bound him in bronze shackles to take him away to Baḇel.
And Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar brought some of the utensils from the House of HWHY to Baḇel and put them in his hĕḵal at Baḇel.

And the rest of the acts of Yehoyaqim, the abominations which he did and what was found against him, see, they are written in the book of the sovereigns of Yasharal and Yahuḏah. Then Yehoyaḵin his son reigned.
Yehoyaḵin was eight years old when he began to reign and he reigned in Yerushalayim three months and ten days. He did evil in the eyes of HWHY.

And at the turn of the year Sovereign Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar sent and brought him to Baḇel, with the valuable utensils from the House of HWHY and made Tsiḏqiyahu, Yehoyaqim’s brother, sovereign over Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim.
Tsiḏqiyahu was twenty-one years old when he began to reign and he reigned Aleven years in Yerushalayim.
He did evil in the eyes of HWHY his Aluahim. He did not humble himself b e fo r e Y ir m e y a h u t h e p r o ph e t , w ho sp o k e f ro m t h e m o u t h o f H W H Y .
He also rebelled against Sovereign Neḇuḵaḏnetstsar, who had made him swear by Aluahim, but he stiffened his neck and hardened his heart against turning to HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal.

Also, all the heads of the kohanim and the people trespassed more and more, according to all the abominations of the gentiles and they defiled the House of HWHY which He had set-apart in Yerushalayim.
And HWHY Aluahim of their fathers sent to them, by His mal'akim, rising up early and sending them, for He had compassion on His people and on His dwelling place.
But they were mocking the mal'akim of Aluahim and despising His words and scoffing at His neḇi'im, until the wrath of HWHY arose against His people, until there was no healing.

Therefore He brought against them the sovereign of the Chaldeans and he slew their young men with the sword in the House of their set-apart place and had no compassion on young man or maiden, on the aged or the weak – He gave all into his hand.
And all the utensils from the House of Aluahim, great and small and the treasures of the House of HWHY and the treasures of the sovereign and of his leaders, all these he brought to Baḇel.
And they burned the House of Aluahim and broke down the wall of Yerushalayim and burned all its palaces with fire and destroyed all its valuable utensils.
And those who escaped from the sword he exiled to Baḇel, where they became servants to him and his sons until the reign of the reign of Persia,
in order to fill the Word of HWHY by the mouth of Yirmeyahu, until the land had enjoyed her Shabbaths. As long as she lay waste she kept Shabbath, until seventy years were completed.
